name: eviction-defense
description: Tenant rights, eviction timelines, legal defenses, and emergency resources for people facing housing loss. The acute crisis pack — not long-term housing programs.
source: Liberation Labs mutual aid knowledge base

triples.subject: pay-or-quit notice
triples.object: minimum statutory notice period (typically 3-30 days depending on state)

triples.subject: tenant
triples.object: habitability defense — landlord's failure to maintain safe/livable conditions can defeat eviction for nonpayment

triples.subject: eviction filing
triples.object: right to file an answer within the statutory deadline (typically 5-14 days)

triples.subject: illegal lockout
triples.object: emergency court order for re-entry — landlords cannot change locks, remove belongings, or shut off utilities without court order

triples.subject: rental assistance application
triples.object: eviction proceedings in many jurisdictions while application is pending

triples.subject: retaliatory eviction
triples.object: filed within protected period after tenant complained about code violations, organized tenants, or exercised legal rights

triples.subject: tenant
triples.object: cure period — opportunity to pay overdue rent and stop the eviction before judgment

triples.subject: eviction judgment
triples.object: within statutory window (typically 5-30 days), sometimes requiring bond for rent during appeal

triples.subject: notice to quit
triples.object: correct tenant name, property address, specific reason for eviction, and comply with service requirements

triples.subject: improper notice
triples.object: dismissal of eviction case — technical defects in notice are a common defense

triples.subject: self-help eviction
triples.object: changing locks, removing doors/windows, shutting off utilities, removing belongings — all illegal in every US state

triples.subject: tenant
triples.object: all communications with landlord, condition of property, repairs requested, and payments made — photos with timestamps

triples.subject: legal aid organization
triples.object: free eviction defense for income-qualifying tenants — most cities have at least one

triples.subject: court appearance
triples.object: failure to appear results in default judgment for landlord even if tenant has valid defenses

triples.subject: section 8 voucher holder
triples.object: landlord cannot terminate tenancy solely because tenant uses housing voucher (in most jurisdictions)

triples.subject: lease violation eviction
triples.object: landlord to prove the specific violation occurred AND that it's material enough to warrant termination

triples.subject: covid-era protections
triples.object: most federal protections expired but some state/local protections remain — check local rules

triples.subject: tenant
triples.object: reporting health/safety violations to authorities (protected activity in all states)

triples.subject: mobile home tenant
triples.object: many states have specific mobile home tenant acts with longer notice periods and relocation assistance

triples.subject: emergency shelter
triples.object: 211 hotline, local homeless services, domestic violence hotlines (if applicable), or faith-based organizations

triples.subject: eviction record
triples.object: in some states, especially if tenant won the case or it was dismissed

triples.subject: just cause eviction ordinance
triples.object: many cities — limits eviction to specific enumerated reasons even for month-to-month tenancies

triples.subject: constructive eviction
triples.object: landlord's failure to maintain property makes it uninhabitable, effectively forcing tenant to leave

triples.subject: tenant organizing
triples.object: state and federal law — landlords cannot retaliate against tenants for organizing or joining tenant unions

triples.subject: eviction moratorium
triples.object: declared emergencies — check local emergency orders for temporary protections
